Active Planned Care / GoalsStart DateReason
Dementia management27-Oct-2018Alzheimer's disease (disorder)
  • Patient will maintain orientation to person, place, and time as much as possible.
  • Patient will retain independence in basic activities of daily living such as bathing, dressing, and feeding.
  • Reduce frequency and severity of agitation, aggression, or other behavioral symptoms.
  • Patient or caregiver will administer dementia medications as prescribed.
  • Caregiver will receive education and support to manage patient’s dementia-related needs.
Lifestyle education regarding hypertension12-Aug-2006Essential hypertension (disorder)
  • Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
  • Patient will adhere to recommended dietary, physical activity, and substance use modifications to support blood pressure control.
Care plan30-Aug-2004-
Diabetes self management plan29-Mar-1985Prediabetes (finding)
  • Patient will attend diabetes self-management education sessions to improve understanding of prediabetes and lifestyle modifications.
  • Maintain fasting plasma glucose within recommended range for prediabetes.
  • Maintain HbA1c below threshold for diabetes diagnosis.
  • Achieve and maintain weight loss as recommended for prediabetes management.
Asthma self management27-Feb-1941Childhood asthma (disorder)
  • Patient will demonstrate understanding of asthma self-management techniques, including inhaler use, trigger avoidance, and symptom monitoring.
  • Patient will report no more than two episodes of asthma symptoms per week.
  • Patient will take prescribed asthma medications as directed, with at least 90% adherence.
  • Patient will monitor and record peak expiratory flow rate daily.
Immunization RecommendationDue DateReason
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product01-Oct-2026Annual seasonal influenza vaccination
